short_summary: El Niño active, wildfire risk sustained
rationale: El Niño is now formally confirmed active again this cycle, sustaining elevated wildfire and drought risk for Iberia — the most directly relevant threat to a Portuguese resident's daily life.

top_events.title: El Niño confirmed underway, threatens weather extremes
top_events.summary: Scientists confirm El Niño is active; for Portugal this means elevated risk of drought, heatwaves, and severe wildfire season — a direct and serious seasonal threat to life and property.

top_events.title: Trump claims US-Iran settlement, markets surge
top_events.summary: Potential easing of oil prices from an Iran deal could modestly reduce energy and transport costs for Portuguese consumers, but impact is indirect and unconfirmed.

top_events.title: Pope Leo visits Canary Islands highlighting migrant journeys
top_events.summary: Pope's visit draws attention to Atlantic migration routes; relevant to Portugal given proximity and shared Atlantic migration pressure, but no immediate policy change.
